blocks.text: looked at the water. "Any time," she said, like it was nothing. She made it nothing so he didn't have to carry it.
understood the silence. She'd learned to read the ones that meant yes.
said "obviously" without looking up. He smiled. She didn't see it.
let the quiet run. She was good at that with him.

blocks.content: By the time they walked over, the fire was properly going — orange and low, the kind you build when you know what you're doing. Someone had dragged chairs into a loose circle. Sparkling cider cans on the cooler lid. Music still coming from the speaker but quieter now, background.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You made it. Grab something to drink — it's all on the cooler.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: Fair warning — Marcus told us you cook, so now he wants to do a whole thing tomorrow. He's been planning it for twenty minutes.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I said maybe we could do a joint situation. That's not a plan, that's an idea.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: What were you thinking?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I don't know yet. I was going to ask what you had in mind.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Cast iron. Whatever's fresh at the marina store in the morning.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Done. That's the plan.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: It became a plan. I saw it happen.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: I'm going in the water tomorrow before it gets warm. Anyone who wants to come — seven-thirty, no later.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: You said that yesterday.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: And I meant it yesterday. Tomorrow I'm actually doing it.

It was easy. That was the thing about them — they'd been doing this long enough that there was nothing to prove. The fire, the cooler, the way nobody filled every silence. I'd been here twenty minutes and I already felt like I'd been here longer.
Wren was in the chair furthest from the fire, turned slightly so the light didn't hit her face directly. She had a drink and she was watching the water. She hadn't said anything since they'd arrived.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Where are you two based?

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: DC. You?

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Charlotte. Same as most of us — Marcus dragged everyone from a three-state radius.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I prefer to call it organizing.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: You called us four months in advance.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Good weekends don't happen by accident.

blocks.prompt: An hour in. The fire's warm, the group is easy. Who does Julian keep coming back to?

options.label: Marcus — he made every person feel like they were the reason he came outside.
Wren — she hasn't said a word in an hour and nobody's pushed her on it.
Nadia — useful before anyone asked. She knew exactly how to come across.
